<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/postgres/02-isolation/no-dirty-reads.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

```
A> BEGIN;
BEGIN

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 999 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1
```

*B explicitly requests READ UNCOMMITTED — the one level that would permit dirty reads.*

```
B> BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED;
BEGIN

B> SELECT current_setting('transaction_isolation') AS isolation;
    isolation     
------------------
 read uncommitted 
(1 row)

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

B> COMMIT;
COMMIT

A> ROLLBACK;
ROLLBACK
```

<small>Verified against PostgreSQL 18.4 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/postgres/02-isolation/no-dirty-reads.ts)</small>
